title: Winning the war to exploit our EEZ
sub_topics: Aquino III initiating arbitration under UNCLOS
International Court of Justice consent requirement
freedom of navigation enforcement by great powers

stances.claim: To win negotiation, one must know the other side's arguments, stance, strengths, and weaknesses — Duterte must approach Xi armed with this strategic knowledge of China's legal positions.
stances.rhetorical_move: negotiation principle stated as truism + strategic framing

stances.claim: Asking China to honor freedom-of-navigation in our territorial seas is harder than asking it to allow Philippine resource exploitation — the latter can be done via China's implied acceptance of our EEZ entitlements, avoiding the China-abhorred Arbitral Award.
stances.rhetorical_move: comparative difficulty analysis + face-saving diplomatic strategy

stances.claim: Let the world powers tangle with China over the freedom-of-navigation portion of the Award; the Philippines should focus on its national interest and resource extraction with or without the Award.
The Filipino people should unite in supporting and praying for the President as he 'tiptoes' to his talks with Xi.
stances.rhetorical_move: appeal to national unity + spiritual framing

cases: Republic of the Philippines v. People's Republic of China (2016 Arbitral Award)
laws_treaties: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS)
events: China's submission of nine-dash-line map to UN (May 7, 2009)

signature_phrases: lose face to its people, something sacred in their culture
as he tiptoes to his talks
register_markers: as I explained in my column last Sunday
decision_framework_signals: applies negotiation-theory 'know the other side' principle to bilateral diplomacy
uses face-saving culture lens to design a workaround that achieves substance without forcing public Chinese reversal
frames enforcement as a portfolio problem: let great powers handle freedom-of-navigation, we focus on resource extraction
cross_references: as I explained in my column last Sunday
